failures.test: byok-flow.spec.ts > BYOK key flow > Settings BYOK actions keep UX clear for saved key vs raw key
failures.error: expect(locator).toBeEnabled() failed — getByRole('button', { name: /test (new|saved) key/i }) is disabled (timeout 5000ms). Button resolved to <button disabled ... data-testid='byok-test'>Test Saved Key</button>
failures.hypothesis: State contamination: Test #1 ('AI page is gated') deletes ALL BYOK keys via API in its setup. Test #2 runs next and expects a saved key to exist (button should say 'Test Saved Key' and be enabled). After deletion, no key exists so button is disabled.
failures.classification: test-bug — state contamination between tests in same describe block
failures.suggested_fix: Add a beforeEach that creates a BYOK key before Test #2, OR restructure tests with test.describe.configure({mode:'serial'}) and add explicit key-creation step. Do NOT use test.skip() — this is a real UX behavior worth testing.

notes: Full suite previously ran 96 tests (84 passed, 1 failed, 11 skipped) per earlier session. The failure is consistently in byok-flow tests due to state contamination. Confirmed reproduced in isolation run.
